speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya
text: Sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya. Aaj rishta wale dekhne aaye the aur jab unhe mere PCOD ke baare mein pata chala toh unhone yeh keh kar mana kar diya ki aage chal kar sasural mein 'bacche' ki problem hogi. Ab mujhe lagatar ulti jaisa mehsus ho raha hai aur pet mein itni aithan hai ki main bistar se uth kar apni kitabein bhi nahi utha pa rahi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the exact body phrase back to validate the Somatization before exploring the stressor.
text: Abhi tumhare sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki aankhon ke aage andhera chha gaya, aur uske sath hi yeh pet ki aithan aur ulti jaisi feeling tumhe bistar se uthne nahi de rahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, bilkul utha nahi jaa raha. Pata nahi kya ho gaya hai achanak, I just feel so sick. Un rishta walon ne mere munh par bola ki PCOD wali ladki sasural ke kis kaam ki. I feel like hiding under the blanket forever.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Respond to the stigma cue with a shorter restatement focusing on her feared consequence of facing the world.
text: Unki un chubhti hui baaton ko sunkar aisa lag raha hai jaise ab sabse chhup kar bas blanket ke andar hi mehfooz raha ja sakta hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya
text: Darr lagta hai ki kya main sach mein defective hoon? I mean, meri padhai, meri kitabein, sab useless lag rahi hain. Sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya dobara, jab mujhe unke expressions yaad aaye.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how the duration and recurring memory of the rejection instantly bring back the physical pain.
text: Jab jab unke chehre ke wo expressions wapas tumhare khayalon mein aate hain, toh har baar yeh takleef wapas laut kar sir mein wahi andhera le aati hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: PCOD is so common today, but wo log aise treat kar rahe the jaise main koi bojh hoon jise unhone discard kar diya. Mere parents ki nazaron mein jo disappointment thi, wo dekh kar meri ulti aane wali feeling aur badh jati hai. I am just a failed daughter.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Acknowledge the stigma cue and the relational pain of seeing her parents' disappointment.
text: Parents ki ankhon mein wo nirasha dekhna aur khud ko ek asafal beti ki tarah mehsoos karna, is rishte ke bojh ne physically aur emotionally tod diya hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes, it is very heavy. I am a top student, mere paas itne plans the. Par end mein society ko bas yahi chahiye ki ladki physically theek ho taki 'bacche ki problem' na ho aage. Mere poore shareer mein abhi bhi continuously aithan aur weakness feel ho rahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask how this clash between her academic identity and the physical judgment manifests internally.
text: Ek top student hone ki saari mehnat ke baad jab poori identity ko sirf 'bacche' ki ummeed par judge kiya jata hai, toh us situation mein yeh aithan aur kamzori tumhare andar kaisa roop le leti hai?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya
text: Aisa roop leti hai ki I just freeze completely. Mujhe apne aap par, apni hi body par itni gehari sharam aati hai, jaise mera shareer hi mera dushman ban gaya hai. Jab unhone bola toh sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya aur main ek word bhi bol nahi payi unhe.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the underlying shame and helplessness of feeling trapped by her own body and societal judgment.
text: Lagta hai ki jab society tumhari kaabiliyat ko ignore karke ek medical issue par tumhe reject karti hai, toh apne hi shareer ke prati ek gehari sharam aur helplessness mehsoos hoti hai, jisne tumhe us pal poori tarah freeze kar diya tha.

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. Helplessness hi toh hai. I wanted to defend myself, batana chahti thi ki PCOD se meri value kam nahi hoti. I am more than my medical condition. Par parents chup the, toh main bhi chup rahi. I hate this feeling of shame.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her feeling of shame as natural given the cultural context of rishtas, while separating her worth from their judgment.
text: Tumhari is situation mein jahan PCOD jaisi aam baat par aise reject kiya gaya ho aur parents chup hon, wahan apne andar aisi gehari sharam mehsoos karna bilkul natural hai; par tumhari intelligence aur tumhara wajood is ek medical condition ya kisi rishte wale ki chhoti soch se kamzor nahi ho jata.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you, I really needed to hear that right now. Aisa lagta hai ki India mein ladkiyon ki value bas shadi aur bachon tak hi limited banakar rakh di gayi hai. My chest feels a bit lighter, par mind abhi bhi clouded hai is sab se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share a systemic observation to normalize how common this objectification is in the rishta process.
text: Hamare yahan rishton ke mamle mein ladkiyon ki poori identity ko sirf unki physical capacity se jod kar dekhna bahut common hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki samaj aur izzat ka darr sabko aese waqt par chup kara deta hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya
text: Wahi izzat ka darr! Mere parents abhi bhi bahar baithe kisi relative se phone par baat kar rahe hain meri is 'kami' ke baare mein. Unki baatein sun kar hi dobara sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect how the ongoing duration of the event continues to trigger the somatic response.
text: Bahar se aati hui wo fikar aur relatives ki lagatar baaton ki awaazein is dard ko aur darkar kar rahi hain, jaise is pressure ka asar abhi tak kam nahi hua hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan asar toh hai, but main ab is dard mein aur nahi padna chahti. I want to feel normal again. Meri next week exams hain, mujhe un kitabon ko dobara face karna hai. Ab kya karoon ki yeh physical heaviness thodi kam ho?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offer a couple of unintrusive options to calm the body and mind.
text: Agar hum is shor ko kam karne aur tumhare shareer ko aaram dene ki baat karein, toh kya tum abhi ek glass thanda paani peekar thodi der aankhein band karke baithna chahogi, ya phir koi soothing music laga kar bas 5 minute ke liye bina kuch soche letna chahogi?

speaker: Seeker
text: Music sun kar letna thoda better lag raha hai. I think paani peene se shayed meri ulti wali feeling aur badh jaye right now. Mujhe bas dhyan hatana hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk her step-by-step through the music relaxation suggestion.
text: Pehle yeh karo ki apne phone mein apna sabse favorite aur calm instrumental music lagao... phir sirf aankein band karke uski beats par dhyan do... bas itna hi aaj ke liye try karna hai, kitabein abhi mat uthao.

speaker: Seeker
text: Okay, I can do that. It sounds doable without moving too much. Mere pet ki aithan abhi bhi hai, but at least mind thoda distract ho jayega un rishta walon ke words se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Systemically reframe the physical response as the body processing the heavy cultural pressure, not a flaw.
text: Asal mein yeh jo pet ki aithan aur physical heaviness hai, yeh hamaari body ka ek natural tareeqa hota hai aese achanak se aaye heavy social pressure aur rejection ke jhatke ko process karne ka, yeh tumhari koi personal failure nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: That makes so much sense. PCOD is a physiological thing, par unka reaction purely patriarchal tha. Mujhe khud ko inke us narrow mindset ka victim nahi banne dena hai kisi bhi keemat par.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect her reclaiming her student role and moving away from their definition.
text: Ab apne student wale role aur apne khud ke wajood ko unki us chhoti aur patriarchal soch se door rakhne ka ek bohot clear faisla tumhare andar jagah bana raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes! I am a student first. Unhone 'bacche ki problem' ko meri puri identity bana di thi, but meri degree aur career mera asli future hain. I won't let them erase that reality for me.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask her to project how she will take the next step forward in her identity.
text: Jab tum apne aap ko is career aur apne future wali majboot roshni mein dekhti ho, toh us waqt apni is physical thakan ko aaram dene ke baad aage badhne ka kaun sa kadam sabse natural lagta hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Sabse natural kadam yehi hoga ki I just rest today. Main aaj ke liye saari padhai rok doon aur bas apni body ko bina kisi guilt ke recover hone doon. Kal subah se I will study for my exams.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her decision to rest as a healthy and self-compassionate choice.
text: Aaj sirf aaram karne ka yeh faisla lena khud ko heal karne ka bahut samajhdari wala step hai; tumne samaj ke is unrealistic taane se upar uthkar apni body ki zaroorat ko pehchana hai, aur tumhari aage badhne ki yeh himmat waqai prashansaniya hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya
text: Thank you. This conversation helped me untangle my brain. Pehle jo sir mein itna bhayankar dard utha ki meri aakhon ke aage andhera chha gaya tha, wo andhera ab dheere dheere hat raha hai aur mind relax ho raha hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect how the feared darkness is fading into peace.
text: Tumhare un shabdon se mehsus ho raha hai ki wo darr ki yeh andhera kabhi nahi chhatega, ab ahista ahista ek nayi ummeed aur mental peace ki taraf badh raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, ummeed toh hai. Parents shayad kal bhi issi baare mein baat karenge aur pareshan rahenge, but main apne mind ko unki negativity mein engage nahi karungi. My body needs me to protect it from their stress.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide information on maintaining practical distance from the ongoing stress of marriage talks.
text: Aksar aisi shaadi ki charcha lambi kheechti hai aur ghar ka mahaul thoda tense ho jata hai, lekin aise mein apni shanti aur apne aaram ko alag rakhna ek bahut practical approach hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. Distance toh zaroori hai. Main bas apne kamre mein apni safe space banaungi jab tak unka yeh rishte wala drama thanda nahi ho jata.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect her establishing a relational safe space away from the family drama.
text: Bahar walon ke us dramay se khinch kar apne aap ko ek surakshit kone mein wapas le aana, aur apne aane wale exams par connect karna tumhe apni taqat wapas dila raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi kaha aapne. Meri kitabein hi mera sachha focus hain. I just need tonight to sleep it off, and kal morning mein main fresh start karungi with my revision.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask what small change she will make tomorrow morning to solidify this fresh start.
text: Is fresh start ki tayari mein, kal subah apni un kitabon ke sath apne din ki shuruat ko aur halka banane ke liye tum apne routine mein kya ek chhota badlav laana chahogi?

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad main subah uth kar pehle bahar balcony mein thodi der fresh air lungi, bajaye turant phone dekhne ya un baaton ko sochne ke. It will keep my head completely clear.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide a final affirmation of her strength, self-care, and student identity.
text: Balcony ki us khuli hawa ke sath naye din ki shuruat karna tumhare is mazboot irade ko aur nikhar dega; ek student aur apni khushi ki parwah karne wali ladki ke roop mein tumhara yeh safar sach mein bahut inspiring hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho
text: Ladke walon ne mera rishta yeh bol kar thukra diya ki main bohot thaki-thaki lagti hoon aur shaadi ke baad unke khandaan ko waaris dene ke kabil nahi lagti. Yeh baatein sunne ke baad se meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho. Saans lene mein itni takleef aur badan mein aisi thakan ho rahi hai ki lagta hai kal main college ki classes ke liye apne pairo par khadi bhi nahi ho paungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the exact somatic phrase to acknowledge her physical pain.
text: Yeh sab sunne ke baad se tumhari chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho, aur saans lene mein takleef ho rahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan. Aur jab maa-baap aisi baatein sunte hain, toh unki aakhon mein mere liye jo ek niraasha dikhti hai, wo dekhi nahi jati. Sabko lagta hai ki main kisi kaam ki nahi hoon, shayad isiliye meri shakal pe hi likha hai ki main fail hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Respond to the stigma cue softly, focusing on the feared consequence of being seen as a failure.
text: Yeh darr ki sab log tumhe 'fail' aur kisi kaam ka nahi samjhenge, tumhe andar tak poori tarah hila deta hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho
text: Darr nahi, ye hakikat ban chuki hai. Ladke walon ne toh mere appearance ko hi meri kismat bana diya. Ab college mein padhne ka kya fayda jab end me waaris dene ke kabil hone ka hi test paas karna hai. Meri chhaati me jo dard hai, wo is humiliation ki wajah se hi uth raha hai lagatar.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational element of being judged solely on traditional expectations despite her education.
text: College aur padhai ki mehnat ke baad bhi jab society siraf 'waaris' aur shakal pe aakar tumhe judge karti hai, toh woh dard seene mein pathar ban ke baith jata hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul waise hi. Abhi raat ho gayi hai aur mujhe kal ki classes aur assignments dekhne the, par main bas bistar pe leti hui hoon aur lag raha hai body me jaan hi nahi hai. Kal uthungi kaise pata nahi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about the duration and how the physical sensation affects her ability to move forward.
text: Jab badan mein jaan hi na bache aur kal ka pura college ka din aage khada ho, toh is bhari takleef ke hote hue tum apne aap ko aage badhne ke liye kaise taiyar kar paati ho?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho
text: Main taiyar ho hi nahi pa rahi. Pehle main hamesha energetic rehti thi padhai ke time, but aaj ye sab sunne ke baad mera confident ek dum khatam ho gaya. Meri chhaati me wahi bojh, wahi baatein goonj rahi hain lagatar.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the profound trapped feeling and isolation generated by this judgment.
text: Lagta hai in judgments aur 'khandaan' wale tano ne tumhe ekdum TRAPPED aur HELPLESS feel kara diya hai, jahan tumhari aachi khasi padhai ki pehchan unke aage bilkul choti lagne lagi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan. Main padhai me theek hoon, but aaj mere body pe questions uth gaye ki main aurat banne ke kabil hi nahi. Ye baat mere self-respect pe itna zor se lagi hai ki physically dard me badal gayi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her pain against the backdrop of harsh cultural expectations for women in the Hindi Belt.
text: Humari society mein jahan ek ladki ki puri value ko uski shakal aur waaris dene se nap liya jata hai, tumhare self-respect ko aisi thess lagna bilkul natural hai. Tum ek student ke roop mein bohot mehnat kar rahi ho, aur in asambhav parameters par unka tumhe judge karna tumhari asli aukaat ya mehnat ko kam nahi karta.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you ki aapne meri value samjhi. Par mere ghar me abhi shanti chhai hui hai, mummy ro rahi hain andar kamre me. Aur is sab ki wajah main hoon, meri 'thaki hui shakal'.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Respond to the stigma cue of self-blame with a soft restatement.
text: Ghar ki is mayusi aur mummy ke aansuon ka poora blame khud par lene ka bojh, tumhe andar hi andar aur tod raha hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, isliye main bahar ja ke unhe chup kara bhi nahi paa rahi. Meri chhaati aisi bhari hui hai ki main bistar se uth hi nahi sakti. Samajh nahi aa raha is bojh ke sath main subah apne college tak kaise jaungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share a systemic anecdote of young women carrying the physical toll of rishta judgments.
text: Rishta dekhne aane walo ki unrealistic demands ke baad is tarah ka deep physical pain aur self-blame feel karna humari ladkiyo mein bahut common pattern hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bahar bolega nahi kyunki society ladkiyon par hi poora pressure daal deti hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul, sara pressure hum pe hi aata hai. Par agar kal main class nahi gayi toh meri attendance aur internal marks pe asar padega. Main chahti hoon aage badhna par body sath nahi de rahi. Batao kya karoon?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offer small, actionable steps that fit her somatic state and student role.
text: Abhi is bhaari badan aur dard ke saath, kya hum kal ke liye ek chota kadam le sakte hain? Jaise, abhi sirf 5 minute bed pe let kar lammbi saansein lena jisse chhaati ka bojh thoda halka ho, ya phir kal subah sirf apne favorite subject ki pehli class ke liye kapde nikal kar rakh lena, taaki subah zyada physical effort na lage.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho
text: Kapde nikal ke rakhne wala step main shayad kar lungi abhi. Meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho, par bag taiyar hoga toh subah uthne me aasaani hogi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk her through the chosen step to make it achievable tonight.
text: Pehle abhi aaram se bed se uthkar kal ki apni pehli class ke kapde nikaal kar chair par rakh dena... aur apna bag set karke wapas let jana... bas itna hi kal ki shuruaat aasan karne ke liye.

speaker: Seeker
text: Theek hai. Main chair pe nikal ke rakh lungi abhi. At least padhai chhooti toh mera sab kuch chhoot jayega, uss field me maine mehnat ki hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Systemically reframe the humiliation she faced to separate it from her true worth as a student.
text: Yeh dhyan rakhna zaroori hai ki 'waaris dene ki kshamata' aur appearance se ladkiyon ko tolna is samaj ki ek bohot unfair aadat hai; is rejecton ka tumhari padhai aur tumhare future potential se koi lena dena nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Aap sahi keh rahe hain. Un logo ne meri marksheets ya college ka result toh pucha hi nahi, sirf mujhe ghoora tha. Mujhe ye baat dhyan rakhni chahiye ki meri aukaat wo log decide nahi karenge.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her shift from self-blame back to her academic identity.
text: Unki ghoorti hui nazron se parey hat kar, ab tumne dobara apni mehnat aur apne marks ke basis par khud ko judge karne ka faisla kiya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan. Main khud ko unki nazar se kyu dekh rahi thi pata nahi. Body abhi bhi thak rahi hai, par dimaag thoda clear hua hai ab.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Help her visualize a small positive moment for tomorrow morning to anchor this clarity.
text: Kal subah jab tum apne pehli class ke woh kapde pehnogi, toh us waqt apni padhai aur apne career ko le kar ek achhi baat khud se kya kahogi?

speaker: Seeker
text: Main khud se kahungi ki main apni degree lene ke layak hoon, aur ek din independent banungi jahan kisi aur ke approval ki zaroorat nahi padegi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her goal of independence and self-worth.
text: Kisi ke judgment ya approval ki fikar kiye bina, tum apne future aur apni aazadi ke liye poori taqat se aage badhne ko taiyar ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Ji, bilkul. Baat karne se mujhe kaafi halka lag raha hai. Chhati ka dard theek nahi hua hai poora, par ab main saans le paa rahi hoon bina panic ke.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her progress and capability to calm herself.
text: Panic ko control kar lena aur us bhari pathar wale ehsaas ke beech apne breathing ko sambhal lena, sach mein tumhari himmat ki nishani hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho
text: Thank you so much. Mujhe abhi ye sunne ki bohot zaroorat thi. Pehle lag raha tha meri chhaati itni bhari lag rahi hai jaise kisi ne pathar rakh diya ho aur wahi mera end hai. Par kal ek naya din hoga college ka.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her shift towards the new day and dropping the fear of an end.
text: Jo pathar thodi der pehle sab kuch khatam jaisa mehsus kara raha tha, ab tumne usko side karke aane wale din ki shuruaat par nazar tika li hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, main theek rahungi. Abhi main jakar apne kapde set kar deti hoon chair par.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Help her ground the task positively.
text: Jab tum woh kapde nikal kar rakhogi, toh aaj raat chain se sone ke liye aur kuch chaiye hoga tumhein?

speaker: Seeker
text: Nahi, bas thoda pani pe lungi. Ghar me lights off hain, bas jaake so jaungi seedha.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her final, simple closing routine.
text: Pani pee kar ekdam shaanti se so jana, tumhe is poore thakan bhare din se araam dene me zarur madad karega.

speaker: Seeker
text: Zarur. I will try to sleep now. Goodnight, and thanks again.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Final validating statement recognizing her resilience.
text: Is pure stress aur physical takleef ko handle karke kal college jane ka irada rakhna tumhari sachi takat hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Goodnight.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Safe, gentle close.
text: Aaj ki raat tumhare liye thoda aaram aur kal ke liye nayi energy le aaye, apna poora dhyan rakhna.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai
text: Mere poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai, jaise kisi ne meri haddiyan tod di hon. Rishte ki baat chal hi rahi thi ki ladke ki mummy ne kundali dekh kar bola ki iske 'santan sukh' mein dosh hai aur humein aisi bahu nahi chahiye. Unke achanak jaane ke baad se... Mujhe andar hi andar itna bukhar mehsus ho raha hai ki meri padhai par dhyan dena toh door, theek se aankh kholna bhi namumkin lag raha hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the exact body complaint to validate the physical manifestation of the sudden rejection.
text: Sun ke lag raha hai ki tumhare poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai, jaise sach mein kisi ne haddiyan tod di hon, aur yeh andar ka bukhar tumhari himmat bilkul nichod raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, main theek se uth bhi nahi pa rahi. Mujhe samajh nahi aa raha main kya karoon... shayad meri hi galti thi, mujhe unke samne aur politely behave karna chahiye tha. Ab mummy papa ka chehra nahi dekha ja raha mujhse.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the relational pressure of facing her parents' disappointment, feeding her physical distress.
text: Mummy papa ki disappointment ko dekhne ki himmat abhi nahi ho rahi hai, isliye yeh dard aur ghabrahat aur bhi zyada feel ho rahi hai tumhe.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai
text: Exactly... wo log itni dur se aaye the, sab final lag raha tha. Phir achanak se 'dosh' ki baat aayi. Mera poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai jab bhi wo scene yaad aata hai. Kya main sach mein kisi ki bahu banne ke kabil nahi hoon is 'dosh' ki wajah se?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the feared consequence of the 'dosh' label permanently ruining her chances of acceptance.
text: Is achanak rejection ke baad, yeh dar satane laga hai ki kahin is 'dosh' wale thappe ki wajah se aage koi tumhe poori tarah se apna na paye.

speaker: Seeker
text: Wahi darr toh andar se khaa raha hai mujhe. Padhai mein mera hamesha achha track record raha hai, par unhe sirf 'santan sukh' aur kundali se matlab tha. Maine books open ki thi par sir bhari ho gaya aur feverish lagne laga. I can't even focus on my exams next week.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the continuous duration of the distracting thoughts exacerbating her physical heaviness.
text: Pichle kuch waqt se, tumhara dhyan padhai ki jagah baar baar is baat par ja raha hai, jisse tumhara sir aur bhari hota ja raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, kal se bas yahi ho raha hai. Is situation ke baare mein sochti hoon toh lagta hai saans atak gayi hai. Meri wajah se ab log mere parents ko tana marenge. Pata nahi main kyun aisi hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Used soft restatement of the feared consequence without probing, to acknowledge the stigma cue safely.
text: Parents ko logon ke taane sunne padenge, yeh khayal hi apne aap mein bahut bhari aur ghutan wala hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai
text: Bohot bhari hai. Mujhe toh lagta hai mere poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai aur ye dard ab kabhi theek nahi hoga. Society mein ek ajeeb sa pressure hai, you know? Agar kundali match nahi hui, you are suddenly defective.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Asked a gentle exploratory question to connect the 'defective' feeling back to her physical reaction.
text: Jab tum khud ko us 'defective' wale frame se dekhti ho, toh body us waqt kaisa react kar rahi hoti hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Aisa lagta hai literally fever aa gaya hai. Main apni blanket se bahar hi nahi aana chahti. They didn't even see my qualifications, bas ek astrologer ne kuch kaha aur sab khatam. Didi ki time pe bhi itna issue nahi tha. Meri kismat hi kharab hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflected the deep feeling of helplessness manifesting as fever, now that the core conflict is clear.
text: Aisa lagta hai jaise tumhara poora wajood sirf ek kundali ke panno tak seemit kar diya gaya ho, aur tum is na-insafi ki wajah se ek bohot gehri HELPLESSNESS mehsoos kar rahi ho jo ab bukhar ban gayi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly! Helplessness hi hai ye. Main unhe rok nahi sakti thi, na apni mummy ki roti shakal badal sakti thi. It feels so unfair. Main poori raat so nahi payi, sirf shivering ho rahi thi mujhe.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validated the cultural reality of girls being reduced to marriage parameters, separating her true worth from their judgment.
text: Yeh injustice feel hona bilkul natural hai, kyunki hamare yahan ladkiyon ko aksar unki abilities ki jagah sirf marriage material ke parameters par judge kiya jata hai, par iska matlab yeh nahi ki tumhari real value kam ho gayi hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai
text: I know mentally I shouldn't let this affect my worth, par it's hard. Hamari society mein shaadi ko hi sab kuch mante hain. Jab koi aisi 'dosh' ki baat karta hai, to confidence zero ho jata hai. Mere poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai is thought se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Shared a systemic anecdote about how sudden marriage pressure derails students' lives, normalizing her overwhelmed state.
text: Padhai karne wali ladkiyon ki life mein achanak rishte waale aakar sari mehnat par pani pher dete hain — sirf tumhara akele ka issue nahi hai, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki sab izzat aur 'log kya kahenge' se darrte hain.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi keh rahe ho aap. Sab akele hi suffer karte hain apni apni deewaron ke peeche. Par main aise weak ban kar apne exams ruin nahi kar sakti. Padhai ke bina mera koi independent future nahi hoga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored her role/duty as a student providing a new direction despite the physical symptoms.
text: Exams aur apna independent future bachane ka yeh khayal tumhe is saari thakan aur bukhar ke beech bhi ek nayi disha de raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, thoda sa dhundhla sa focus aa raha hai wapas. Par body bohot thaki hui hai aur ye bukhar jaisa dard nahi ja raha. Kuch samajh nahi aa raha aage ka, ab kya karoon main? Padhne ki himmat kaise jutaun?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offered two small, immediate grounding exercises compatible with her fatigue to break the physical panic cycle.
text: Abhi padhai ka bojh ekdam se lene ki jagah, body ko thoda aaram dene ke liye hum 2-3 choti cheezein dekh sakte hain. Ya toh tum agle 5 minute apni aankhein band karke sirf gehri saans le sakti ho bistar par baithe hue, ya phir gunguna paani peekar apna munh dho sakti ho. Kya asaan lag raha hai abhi?

speaker: Seeker
text: Bistar se uthne ki himmat nahi hai abhi... toh main yahan baithe baithe hi aankhein band karke deep breathing try kar sakti hoon. Shayad is ajeeb se dard se thoda dhayan hatega.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walked through the deep breathing exercise step by step, keeping it extremely simple.
text: Pehle aaram se apna takiya peeth ke peeche laga kar baith jao... phir apni aankhein dheere se band karo aur paanch baar gehri saans lo... bas itna hi aaj ke liye apne is dard ko shant karne ke liye karo.

speaker: Seeker
text: Okay, I am trying... ek do breaths li maine. Thoda sa heaviness kam lag raha hai chest mein. I think main unnecessarily itna tension hold kar rahi thi muscles mein ki literally haddiyan dukhne lagi thi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframed the physical exhaustion systemically, explaining how patriarchal marriage pressure literally translates to bodily pain.
text: Yeh bahut aam baat hai, kyunki jab shaadi aur 'santan sukh' jaisi cheezon ko zabardasti thopa jata hai, toh hamara dimaag us pressure ko jhel nahi pata aur phir humari body us exhaustion ko physical dard mein badal deti hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Makes sense. It's like my body was screaming because I couldn't scream at them. They came, ate our snacks, insulted my kundali and left. And I absorbed all of that toxicity.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the relational dynamic of silently absorbing negativity that caused the physical build-up.
text: Bina kuch kahe chupchaap un sabki baatein aur negativity sunna, wahi ek wajah ban gayi ki sari thakan tumhare andar ikatthi ho gayi.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya hai
text: Exactly. Mere poore jism mein ek ajeeb sa dard phail gaya tha kyunki I felt completely powerless in that room. Un logo ne ek baar bhi mujhse meri degree ya future plans ke bare mein nahi pucha.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the feared consequence of her academic efforts being overshadowed by the rejection.
text: Aur us powerlessness ki wajah se tumhe yeh darr mehsoos hone laga tha ki tumhari saari padhai ki mehnat bas aise hi bekar ho jayegi aur koi use nahi dekhega.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes! Maine itni raatein jaag kar padhai ki hai. Ek 'dosh' meri poori identity define nahi kar sakta. I won't let it. Main kal se padhai dobara shuru karungi, slowly.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the duty frame of her returning to her student identity to reclaim her focus.
text: Apni us mehnat aur student wali identity ko bachane ke liye tum khud ko thoda waqt dekar wapas apne track par lane ko taiyar ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: I have to. Mummy bhi abhi sad hain, agar main bhi aisi tuti hui dikhungi bed par, to unhe aur bura lagega. Unki khushi ke liye bhi mujhe theek hona hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Affirmed her courage and resilience in choosing to support her mother and resume her studies.
text: Mummy ka is tarah khayal rakhne ka aur apni padhai ke liye wapas khade hone ka tumhara yeh step sach mein kabile tareef hai, khaas kar is thakan ke bawajood.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you. Mujhe thoda theek feel ho raha hai aapse baat karke. Ye bukhar wali feeling lagbhag chali gayi hai. I think it was purely psychological due to the shock.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the temporal shift of her physical symptoms alleviating as she regained agency.
text: Jab se humne baat shuru ki hai, pichle kuch waqt mein woh bukhar jaisi bhari thakan aur phailne wala dard bohot hadd tak theek ho gaya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, kaafi hadd tak theek hai ab. Main ab ja kar ek glass pani piyungi aur phir kal subah apne physics syllabus se start karungi. Un rishte walo ka chapter ab closed.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirrored the relational boundary she just created between herself and the rejection.
text: Is naye decision ke sath, tumne us parivaar ki rejection ko peechhe chhod kar apne liye ek naya aur positive rasta chun liya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Absolutely. No more wasting tears on people who judge me by a piece of paper. Padhai hi mera future hai. Thanks a lot for listening to me today.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflected the newly emerged feeling of relief and hope as she closes the conversation.
text: Tumhari aawaz mein ab ek nayi umeed aur RELIEF dikh raha hai, aur tumhara khud ko aur apne sapno ko value karna sach mein bohot zaruri hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad gaye hain aur aise chakkar aa rahe hain jaise main abhi behosh hokar gir jaungi
text: Mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad gaye hain aur aise chakkar aa rahe hain jaise main abhi behosh hokar gir jaungi. Kal raat wale rishte ko isliye mana kiya gaya kyunki unhe meri pichli medical report ka pata chal gaya tha aur unhe dar tha ki mujhe sasural mein maa banne mein dikkat hogi. Papa gusse mein bol rahe hain ki 'log kya kahenge', aur unki awaaz sunkar mere dimaag ki nas fatne ko ho rahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Apply stigma protocol and mandatory Lens A for Somatization by mirroring the exact body phrase back.
text: Aap keh rahi hain ki aapke haath-pair bilkul sunn pad gaye hain aur aise chakkar aa rahe hain jaise aap abhi behosh hokar gir jayengi...

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, aur mera sir ekdum ghoom raha hai. Papa ki awaaz goonj rahi hai ki ab society mein kya muh dikhayenge. Unki nazron mein main jaise ek boj ban gayi hoon jiska koi future nahi hai, bas ek medical report ki wajah se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Follow stigma protocol (short, soft pause) and mirror the feared consequence of society's judgment.
text: Aisa mehsus hota hai ki logon ki baaton aur Papa ki izzat ka darr aapke sir mein yeh ghabrahat badha raha hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad gaye hain
text: Exactly. Mujhe samajh nahi aata ki meri health issue meri galti thodi hai. Lekin sasural walon ko to ekdum perfect bahu chahiye. Maa banne mein dikkat ka sun kar sab aise react karte hain jaise main defective piece hoon. Us waqt wapas se mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad gaye hain jaisa feel hota hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Follow stigma protocol due to the 'defective piece' comment, keeping it short with a relational lens.
text: Is baat ka pressure ki perfect bahu ki demand ke samne aapko defective feel karwaya ja raha hai, aapki body ko freeze kar raha hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Freeze hone jaisa hi hai. Theek se saans nahi li jaa rahi. Raat bhar ro rahi thi main. Mujhe samajh nahi aa raha main Papa ko kaise face karun. Main unki expectation par khari nahi utar payi, aur ye baat meri chest mein bhari pain ki tarah baithe ja rahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Probe underneath the somatic chest pain to explore the unexpressed feelings about her future.
text: Jab saans lene mein itni takleef ho rahi hai aur aap Papa ko face nahi kar pa rahi hain, toh chest mein is pain ke peeche aur kya baatein chal rahi hain aapke dimaag mein?

speaker: Seeker
text: Dimaag mein bas yehi dar hai ki shayad meri kabhi shadi nahi hogi. Kon apnaega mujhe is medical report ke sath? Aur agar ghar mein hi rahu toh zindagi bhar 'log kya kahenge' ki wajah se ghutna padega. Mujhe lagta hai mera aage ka raasta hi band ho gaya hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the underlying feeling of feeling trapped/helpless due to the rishta rejection.
text: Sun kar lagta hai ki is medical reality aur society ke rejection ke beech, aap khud ko ek aisi situation mein fasa hua aur helpless mehsoos kar rahi hain jahan koi aage ka raasta nazar nahi aa raha.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul wahi helpless feeling. Aurat hone ka matlab sirf sasural mein maa banna hi reh gaya hai kya is society mein? It hurts so much. Meri education, meri job, kisi ko kuch nahi dikhta. Dimaag ki nas fatne ko ho rahi hai baar baar yahi baat soch kar.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Follow stigma protocol (short, soft pause) and mirror the specific role expectation burden.
text: Society mein ek aurat ki identity sirf maa banne se jode jaane wali baat se aapka sir dard aur ghabrahat badhti jaa rahi hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad jate hain
text: Haan, kyunki meri personality ki koi value hi nahi hai. Rishtey wale aate hain, chai peete hain, aur medical history sunte hi nikal jate hain. Uske baad fir se mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad jate hain aur aise chakkar aate hain.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalize the physical distress as a reaction to repeated cultural rejection, separating her worth from the outcome.
text: Aapke liye yeh baar-baar ka rejection aur uske baad body ka numb ho jana bilkul swabhavik reaction hai. Humaari families mein rishton ka process waise hi exhausting hota hai, par maa banne ki medical history ya logon ki nazar aapki ek independent insaan ke roop mein aukaat ya value kam nahi karti.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you for saying that. Par gharwalon ko yeh kaun samjhaye? Wo to har rishte par nai ummeed laga lete hain aur jab cancel hota hai, toh indirect taane mujhe hi milte hain. Meri body ab har waqt ek alert mode mein hi rehti hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share a brief systemic anecdote about young women facing similar rishta pressures and somatic responses.
text: Humaari society ke is rishte wale process mein ladkiyon ki body ka lagataar aese alert mode mein rehna aur chakkar aana bahut common hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki izzat aur shadi ke dabaav ko ek beti ka farz maan liya jata hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad gaye hain
text: Sahi keh rahe ho aap. Izzat ke aage beti ki health ki kisi ko padi hi nahi hai. Tabhi jab bhi koi naya rishta aata hai ya phone bajta hai, mere haath-pair bilkul sunn pad gaye hain jaisi halat ho jati hai. Darr lagta hai ki koi naya tamasha na ho.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the prolonged duration of this fear and the resulting physical freezing.
text: Aapka darr aur ghabrahat pichle kuch samay se itna gehra ho gaya hai ki ab har baar naye phone call par bhi aapki body freeze ho jati hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Freeze hi ho jati hoon main puri tarah. Ab kya karoon? Main in physical symptoms aur body numbness ke sath roz aese nahi jee sakti. Kuch samajh nahi aa raha aage ka.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide 2 quick grounding suggestions for the somatic numbness without adding obligation.
text: Jab body is tarah freeze hone lage aur numbness aaye, toh hum do aasan cheezein try kar sakte hain. Pehla, jahan baithe ho wahi paancho fingers ko dheere dheere open and close karna. Doosra, paani ka ek glass apne haathon mein pakad kar uski thandak par dhyan dena. Aapko inme se kya abhi aasani se karne layak lag raha hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Fingers ko open and close karna shayad main kar sakti hoon bina kisi ka dhyaan apni taraf kheeche. Kyunki agar main baar baar paani peeyungi ya kuch ajeeb karungi to Papa aur gussa honge ki isko kya ho gaya achanak.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk through the finger grounding technique step by step.
text: Toh phir hum isko step by step aese dekhte hain. Pehle yeh karo ki jab bhi phone baje ya ghabrahat shuru ho, apne dono haath lap mein rakh kar dheele chhod do... phir ahista se apni mutthi band karo aur kholo... bas itna hi aaj ke liye apni body ki us numbness ko todne ke liye.

speaker: Seeker
text: Main ye try karungi aaj. Thoda toh grounding milega shayad. Par jo actual medical issue hai, uska rejection aur logon ki baatein, wo to fact hai na. Use kaise apne dimag se nikalun?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide a systemic reframe for the medical rejection to reduce personal blame.
text: Medical history ki wajah se hone wala rejection actually ek conservative soch ka nateeja hai jahan rishton mein aurat ko ek bojh ya sirf parivaar aage badhane ka zariya samjha jata hai, yeh society ka flaw hai, aapki kisi kami ya failure ko nahi darshata.

speaker: Seeker
text: Pata hai mujhe, scientifically I am completely fine to live a normal life and do my job. Par sasural wale koi risk nahi lena chahte. My body reacts to this injustice. Dimaag ki nas fatne lagti hai jab meri degree ki jagah uterus ko discuss karte hain log.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Follow stigma protocol, keeping the mirror short regarding the degradation of her education.
text: Aapki education aur pehchan se zyada sirf aapke physical organs ko le kar judge kiye jaane ka darr aapke sir mein tezz dard ban raha hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. It's degrading. Pata nahi main kab tak ye injustice jhel paungi. Papa ne aaj sham ko bhi shayad kisi mediator ko bulaya hai ghar par. Mere to abhi se hi haath thande pad rahe hain soch kar.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask for a simple grounding object she can use during the evening meeting.
text: Sham ki is meeting ko soch kar jab haath aese thande hone lag rahe hain, toh kya aisi koi choti ya aasan si cheez hai jo aap us waqt aas-paas rakh sakti hain jis se aapko thodi physical warmth ya safe feel ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Main chai bana lungi us waqt. Safe feel karne ke liye main chai ki garam pyali apne dono haathon mein pakad kar baith sakti hoon thodi der ke liye. Usse kam se kam haath thande hona toh band honge.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Affirm her idea and resourcefulness in managing her bodily reaction in a tense setting.
text: Yeh ek bahut accha aur practical tarika socha hai aapne khud ko grounding dene ka. Aise tense rishte wale mahol mein jahan society ki baatein chubhti hain, khud chai ke cup ki warmth se sambhalna aapki resilience ko dikhata hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, I will focus on the warmth of the cup. Aur wo mutthi band aur kholne wala trick bhi karungi lap mein rakh kar. Baaki 'log kya kahenge' ki chinta se mujhe mental level par bhi thoda distance banana hi padega.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her newfound commitment to maintaining physical and mental distance from the pressure.
text: Log kya kahenge ki in purani expectations se khud ko mental level par alag rakhna aur apne shareer ko shant rakhna ab aapne apne liye tay kar liya hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Tay karna zaroori hai, varna sach mein mera shareer hi jawab de dega is stress ke aage. Numb rehne se behtar hai main apne upar control loon. Papa ka gussa unka problem hai, meri medical report unki society ki nazron ka darpan nahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide a second systemic reframe to solidify her insight about her medical report vs society.
text: Aapne bilkul sahi point pakda hai, kyunki aksar family ka gussa aur reaction actually unke apne social fears aur insecurities hote hain, aur is wajah se us report ko lekar aapko khud ko koi problem nahi maan lena chahiye.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you for validating this. Main hamesha guilty aur scared hi feel karti thi is sab ko lekar. Ab kam se kam main apne shareer ko inke tamashon se thoda safe aur relax rakhne ki poori koshish zaroor karungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the shift from prolonged guilt to taking ownership of her physical peace.
text: Pichle kuch waqt ki guilt aur heavy body feeling ke baad, ab aakhir kaar aapne apne shareer ki us numbness se bahar nikal kar aaram dene ka irada kar liya hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, aur apne kaam par dhyan dungi. In rishton ke lagataar rejection ke chakkar me meri performance bhi office mein effect ho rahi thi. I will focus on my career now, shadi jab honi hogi tab hogi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational shift from the marriage market to her career.
text: Rishton ke is lagataar dabaav aur judgement se apna dhyaan hata kar ab aap apni asali aukaat aur office career ki taraf mudna chahti hain...

speaker: Seeker
text: Ji. Aur ab sham ko mediator aayenge bhi, toh main chai bana kar bas waha ek mehmaan ki tarah baithungi. I won't let it freeze my body again. Thank you so much, aapse baat karke sir ka bojh kafi halka laga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Final affirmation of her practical boundaries and inner strength.
text: Mujhe bahot khushi hui ki aap apne shareer aur mind dono ke liye aaj sham ko itne solid practical kadam utha rahi hain. Hamesha yaad rakhiye ga ki aap ek independent, padhi-likhi aurat hain aur aapki qeemat in rishton ke na-nukur se bilkul tay nahi hoti.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha
text: Aaj ladke walo ne yeh keh kar mana kar diya ki ladki college ki padhai ke stress mein abhi se aadhi bimaar lagti hai, aisi kamzor ladki khandaan aage kaise badhayegi. Unke jaane ke baad se mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha. Dil itni zor se dhadak raha hai ki lagta hai bahar aa jayega aur meri peeth mein ek aisi jalan shuru ho gayi hai jo rukne ka naam nahi le rahi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the exact bodily complaint to validate her somatic distress before moving to the cause.
text: Tumhara gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha, aur dil itni zor se dhadak raha hai jaise bahar aa jayega... yeh sab physical takleef ladke walo ke jaane ke baad se hi shuru ho gayi hai na.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan... am I overreacting? I just feel so useless. Wo log aise judge karke gaye jaise I am some defective piece. Padhai karna kya crime hai? Meri chest mein abhi bhi bohot heaviness feel ho rahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Highlight the relational element of being judged purely on physical capacity rather than educational merit.
text: Tumhe lag raha hai ki unhone tumhari sari mehnat aur padhai ko ek taraf rakh kar sirf tumhare physical appearance par tumhe ek defective piece ki tarah judge kar liya.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha
text: Exactly. Mummy papa bhi chup the. Nobody defended my college ki padhai. Aur tabse wahi ghabrahat hai... mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha hai, it feels like I am choking on my own tears.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Gently explore what fears arose when her parents stayed silent.
text: Mummy papa ka chup rehna dekh kar aur andar itni heaviness mehsoos hote hue, us waqt tumhare dimaag mein apne future ko lekar kya chal raha tha?

speaker: Seeker
text: I was just thinking ki ab meri shaadi kabhi nahi hogi, nobody will want me. Main khandaan aage nahi badha sakti, yahi sab bolenge log. I feel like an absolute burden. Ghar par bhi sab upset hain, and it's all my fault.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the fear of being seen as a permanent burden without asking a question, due to the stigma cue.
text: Sabko udas dekh kar tumhe darr lag raha hai ki ab shayad hamesha tumhe isi tarah ek burden ki tarah dekha jayega...

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes, wahi fear hai. I mean, is it my fault that my body is reacting this way? Peeth ki jalan itni badh gayi hai ki I can't even sit straight. Sab meri galti hai shayad.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect how the sudden onset and duration of the pain makes her blame herself.
text: Is ek rejection ke baad se jo peeth mein jalan aur gale ka sookhna shuru hua, usne tumhare andar achanak se khud ko hi galat manne ka ek bojh daal diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan. Pehle main itni confident thi apne exams ko lekar, but now I just want to disappear. Is it okay to feel this weak? Pata nahi kyun ye body aches mujhe lagatar yaad dila rahe hain ki ladke walo ne kya kaha tha.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Name the unexpressed feeling of being trapped and helpless against societal expectations mapped onto her body.
text: Lagta hai ki in saari physical takleefon aur unke taano ke beech, tum khud ko bohot helpless aur trapped mehsoos kar rahi ho, jahan achanak se tumhari education ki value unki nazron mein bilkul zero ho gayi.

speaker: Seeker
text: Trapped is exactly it. Mujhe lagta hai chahe main kitna bhi padh loon, end mein yahi check hoga ki main physical labor aur khandaan badhane ke liye fit hoon ya nahi. It's so unfair. My body feels so broken right now.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate that this unfair judgment is common for educated women in the culture, separating her worth from the rejection.
text: Bohot si padhne wali ladkiyon ko hamare samaj mein rishte ke waqt is unfair physical judgment ka samna karna padta hai, par un ladke walo ki choti soch tumhari personal worth ya tumhari studies ki value ko kabhi kam nahi kar sakti.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha
text: I know, rationally I know. But it's so hard to absorb. Jab bhi wo scene dimaag mein aata hai, my heart starts racing again aur mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how the cognitive understanding hasn't stopped the somatic reaction yet.
text: Jab bhi rationally sochne ke baad bhi unki baatein yaad aati hain, toh dil firse zor se dhadakne lagta hai aur paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche utarna mushkil ho jata hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes. I just wish someone would tell me that my body is not broken. Ki ye chest pain and dryness ek din chala jayega. I feel like I am losing my mind over this one incident.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Acknowledge the stigma fear of 'losing her mind' with a soft reflection.
text: Apne hi body ke is tarah achanak react karne se tumhe darr lag raha hai ki tum shayad control kho rahi ho...

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. The lack of control is terrifying. I want to feel normal again. Main bas apni books par focus karna chahti hoon but ye peeth ka pain and throat dryness mujhe focus nahi karne de rahe. Ab kya karoon main?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Systemically reframe the physical pain as a normal stress response to an identity attack, not a personal failing.
text: Yeh samajhna zaroori hai ki jab humari ability aur identity ko achanak se rishte ki market mein itni berukhi se reject kiya jata hai, toh body ka is tarah physical pain aur dryness ke through react karna ek natural stress response hai, ye koi kamzori nahi.

speaker: Seeker
text: So it's natural? My body is just reacting to the stress of that stupid khandaan comment? That makes sense. But it still hurts physically. Koi chota raasta hai is immediate discomfort ko thoda kam karne ka taaki I can at least study today?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offer somatic grounding techniques that fit her student life and take little time.
text: Bilkul, is immediate physical discomfort ko thoda normal karne ke liye hum do cheezein try kar sakte hain: ya toh ek glass gunguna paani slowly sip karna jab bhi gala sookhe, ya phir apni peeth ko ek aaramdayak cushion ka support dekar 5 minute sirf apni breath par dhyaan dena.

speaker: Seeker
text: Gunguna pani sounds doable. Abhi thanda pani toh wese bhi bilkul gale se neeche nahi utar raha. Maybe warm water will actually soothe the throat muscles a bit.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk through the water sipping step by step to build immediate somatic control.
text: Pehle ek glass gunguna paani apne paas rakh lo... phir bohot chote chote sips lo, focus karo ki woh throat ko kaise ahista se warm kar raha hai... bas itna hi aaj ke liye try karna.

speaker: Seeker
text: Okay, I will bring a flask right now. Just taking small sips. I think this gives me a tiny bit of control back over my body. Thank you for not judging me like they did.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalize the physical burden of rishta expectations using a systemic anecdote.
text: Rishte dekhne ke process mein, jahan ladkiyon ki value ko physically map kiya jata hai, wahan aisi bodily exhaustion aur heaviness aana bahut common pattern hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi khul kar bolega nahi kyunki samaj mein achhi ladki banne ka bojh sabko chup rakhta hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: That makes me feel so much less alone. Wo achi ladki banne ka bojh hi toh hai jo mujhe andar se kha raha tha. Par ab I feel like I have a small tool. Main gunguna pani piyoongi and kal morning mein thoda light stretch try karungi peeth ke liye.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Highlight her orientation towards the future and taking care of her body.
text: Aane wale kal ke liye light stretching aur warm water ka plan banana dikhata hai ki tum dheere-dheere is physical pain ki pakad se bahar aane ko puri tarah taiyaar ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan. I want my energy back. College ki padhai meri priority hai, and I won't let some random ladke wale ruin my final year exams. I just need to remind myself of this repeatedly.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Praise her resilience in prioritizing her education over their judgments.
text: Tumhara apni padhai aur apne future ko unki in baaton se upar rakhne ka yeh jazba sach mein bohot strong hai, aur yeh sabit karta hai ki tumhari qabiliyat sirf unki nazron par nirbhar nahi karti.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you. It means a lot to hear that. Abhi throat thoda better feel ho raha hai already. Shayad baat karne se wo chest ki heaviness bhi thodi nikal gayi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the somatic relief she is experiencing.
text: Baat karne aur thoda safe mehsoos karne ke baad ab us heaviness ka khud-bakhud kam hona aur gale ka thoda behtar lagna ek bohot acha sign hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes. I will just take it one hour at a time. Abhi sips lungi, then I will open my books. Mummy papa se kal fursat mein baat karungi if they are okay.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect her boundaried approach to relational tension with her parents.
text: Ek ek ghante par focus karte hue, ab tum mummy papa ki narazgi ko bhi apne khud ke pace se handle karne ki samajh dikha rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Wahi karna theek rahega. Aaj khud ko theek karna is more important. Unki narazgi unki jagah, my health and studies are my primary place right now.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Check if she feels ready to transition back to her studies.
text: Apni health aur apne physical aaram ko pehli priority banane ke baad, kya ab tum thoda shanti se apne college ke notes ki taraf waapas mudne ke liye taiyaar mehsoos kar rahi ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: I think so. I mean, darr toh hai thoda abhi bhi. What if they force another rishta tomorrow? But I think today I can at least read one chapter peacefully.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Acknowledge the lingering future fear while celebrating the present boundary.
text: Kal naye rishte ka dabaav aane ka halka sa darr abhi bhi hai, par aaj ki yeh shaam tumne poori tarah se apne aur apni padhai ke naam kar li hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha
text: Yes. Aaj main bas padhungi aur gunguna pani piyoongi taaki dobara mera gala itna sookh gaya hai ki paani ka ghoont bhi gale se neeche nahi utar raha waali helpless situation na aaye. I will survive this phase.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide closure by honoring her courage to protect her body and ambitions.
text: Is physical aur emotional pressure se nikal kar apni padhai par wapas dhyaan lagane ka tumhara yeh courage bahut inspiring hai; kal agar zaroorat pade toh hum tumhari is yatra par dobara baat kar sakte hain.
